target: matcalc (the property-calculator layer that drives MLIPs) and the ASE delta beyond Potential-hosting, as exercised by AtomisticSkills (arXiv 2605.24002).
anchoring_caveat: matcalc is NOT importable in the miniconda base env; its sources were pip-downloaded (matcalc-0.5.1 sdist) and read directly, so matcalc claims are package-source-backed. ASE 3.26.0 IS importable and every ASE unit / class claim was checked live. The AtomisticSkills repository is NOT present on this machine (searched Development/, Development/science/; not found). AtomisticSkills usage of matcalc and ASE is therefore anchored to the three prior committed scans that DID read it directly (scans/mlip-family-atomistic-skills, scans/pymatgen-atomistic-skills, scans/atomate2-vasp-atomistic-skills) plus docs/data/codes.json, NOT to a live read of the skill sources in this scan. A future re-run with the AtomisticSkills repo mounted should confirm the exact matcalc kwargs (units_GPa, fmax, ensemble) each mat-* skill passes.

rail_vs_schemes_recommendation.verdict: schemes-on-operators, NOT a separate rail (same treatment as atomate2).
rail_vs_schemes_recommendation.one_line: matcalc is to MLIPs what atomate2 is to VASP: a workflow / discretization layer. atomate2 got NO separate rail (its makers live in operator-spec schemes); matcalc should get the same. But matcalc is a HARDER case than atomate2 because its fitting steps ARE physics, not mere orchestration.
evidence_for_schemes: matcalc never transforms a value into a NEW physical unit basis of its own: E/F/S come straight from the ASE calculator (atoms.get_potential_energy / get_forces / get_stress), phonons come straight from phonopy, kappa straight from phono3py, EOS bulk modulus straight from pymatgen BirchMurnaghan.b0_GPa, elastic VRH straight from pymatgen ElasticTensor. Every mapped node it 'produces' is already grounded by a rail (MLIP Potential/E/F/S, phonopy, phono3py, pymatgen).

the calculators are thin ASE/phonopy/pymatgen orchestrators: RelaxCalc wraps ase.optimize + FrechetCellFilter; MDCalc wraps ase.md integrators + TrajectoryWriter; PhononCalc wraps phonopy.Phonopy; Phonon3Calc wraps phono3py.Phono3py; EOSCalc wraps pymatgen BirchMurnaghan; ElasticityCalc wraps pymatgen DeformedStructureSet + ElasticTensor.

atomate2 precedent: the orchestrator already decided the VASP-workflow layer is an OperatorRepresentationSpec on solve_ground_state, not a rail. matcalc is the exact structural analog for the MLIP side.
evidence_that_complicates_it: matcalc's strain-stress fit is NOT pure pass-through. ElasticityCalc._elastic_tensor_from_strains does the np.polyfit(strain, stress, 1) itself (matcalc _elasticity.py:182-224), building C_ij from a linear fit over its OWN DeformedStructureSet (norm_strains (-0.01,-0.005,0.005,0.01), shear_strains (-0.06,-0.03,0.03,0.06)). That discretization (which strains, whether to relax deformed cells, use_equilibrium) IS the compute_elastic_constants operator's discretization scheme, not pymatgen's and not the MLIP's.

EOSCalc runs its OWN volume scan (n_points=11 over +/-0.1 max_abs_strain, apply_strain per point, fixed-volume relaxation each point) before handing E(V) to pymatgen's BM fit. The scan grid is a matcalc scheme.

So: matcalc carries genuine discretization schemes (strain grid, EOS volume grid, supercell/mesh/displacement, optimizer/fmax/cell-filter) that belong ON the operator representation, exactly as atomate2's INCAR flags do. This REINFORCES schemes-on-operators (the schemes are real and matcalc-owned) rather than arguing for a rail.
rail_vs_schemes_recommendation.recommended_encoding: Encode matcalc as an OperatorRepresentationSpec (a discretization/scheme layer) on the relevant operators: compute_elastic_constants (ElasticityCalc: strain grid + relax_deformed + use_equilibrium schemes), an EOS/bulk_modulus operator (EOSCalc: volume-scan grid + BM eos-model scheme), the phonon operators (PhononCalc: atom_disp + supercell min_length + symprec schemes, driving the already-mapped phonopy rail), thermal conductivity (Phonon3Calc: fc2/fc3 supercell + mesh schemes, driving phono3py), and relax/MD (RelaxCalc/MDCalc: optimizer + fmax + cell_filter + ensemble schemes). Provenance of every derived node must record BOTH the matcalc driver+scheme AND the MLIP checkpoint (the same double-provenance the mlip scan flagged: 'matcalc is the true producer' via the MLIP PES).

ase_delta_summary: ASE has ONE minimal entry on the map today: {ase: {Potential: {api: ase.Atoms.calc}}}. AtomisticSkills (and matcalc) exercise far more of ASE than Potential-hosting. The delta candidates below are what ASE ITSELF produces beyond hosting a calculator: a relaxed Structure (via ase.optimize + FrechetCellFilter), an MD Trajectory (via ase.md integrators + TrajectoryWriter), and the raw E/F/S the calculator exposes through ASE's get_potential_energy/get_forces/get_stress interface (already mapped as the MLIP nodes, but the ASE interface is the actual production API).
canonical_units_note: matcalc emits ASE units at its PES boundary (E eV/cell, F eV/A, S eV/A^3) and pymatgen/phonopy units at its analysis boundary. Elastic tensor + moduli default to eV/A^3 (units_GPa=False); GPa mode uses pymatgen's ElasticTensor.GPa_to_eV_A3 = 160.21766339999996 (CODATA-2018/2019-exact). matcalc.units.eVA3ToGPa = scipy.constants.e/(A^3*giga) = 160.21766339999996 (SAME CODATA-2018 generation as pymatgen). ase.units.GPa = 160.21766208 (CODATA-2014). So matcalc sits on the pymatgen/CODATA-2018 side of the ~1e-8 in-repo constant split, and ASE (which matcalc's raw stress rides on) sits on the CODATA-2014 side. EOS bulk modulus is bm.b0_GPa (pymatgen, GPa). Phonopy under matcalc: frequencies linear THz; thermal_properties are MOLAR (free_energy kJ/mol, entropy J/K/mol, heat_capacity J/K/mol). Phono3py under matcalc: kappa W/(m*K), spatially averaged over the 3 diagonal components.

entries.physics_route: ASE geometry optimization: ase.optimize optimizer (default FIRE) drives atoms (wrapped in FrechetCellFilter for cell relaxation) to force convergence fmax=0.1 eV/A; returns a relaxed pymatgen Structure plus energy/forces/stress/max_force/is_converged and lattice params.
entries.notes: The equilibrium-structure producer that EVERY other matcalc calc runs first (_prerelax in _base.py:91-145 aborts the chain if the pre-relaxation does not converge). Its final_structure is the Structure node; the relaxed cell is what elastic/EOS/phonon then deform. relax_cell uses FrechetCellFilter (an ASE filter). This is a Structure-producing OPERATION (relax operator), distinct from the opaque input Structure.

entries.api: run_ase(...) -> SimulationResult(structure, potential_energy, kinetic_energy, energy, forces, stress); atoms.get_potential_energy()/get_forces()/get_stress(voigt=False)
entries.physics_route: matcalc reads the SAME three PES quantities off the ASE calculator that the MLIP wrappers expose: E (eV/cell), F (eV/A), S (eV/A^3). run_ase calls get_stress(voigt=False), i.e. a 3x3 tensor (NOT a Voigt-6), which is what ElasticityCalc feeds to pymatgen's strain-stress fitter.
entries.notes: Same nodes the MLIP scan already grounds (mlip-total-energy/forces/stress). matcalc is a CONSUMER of these, not a new producer, but it is the API surface through which the mat-* skills obtain them for the derived quantities. Stress is ASE tension-positive here (3x3), the ASE convention; the MLIP scan verified all three MLIP calculators emit tension-positive stress. The map store Stress is compression-positive (QE/VASP convention), so a sign flip lives between the ASE stress and the map store, as the pymatgen and VASP scans both noted.

entries.api: ElasticityCalc(calc, norm_strains=(-0.01,-0.005,0.005,0.01), shear_strains=(-0.06,-0.03,0.03,0.06), fmax=0.1, use_equilibrium=True, units_GPa=False).calc(struct) -> {elastic_tensor, bulk_modulus_vrh, shear_modulus_vrh, youngs_modulus, residuals_sum, r2_score_mean}
entries.physics_route: strain-stress fitting. matcalc builds a pymatgen DeformedStructureSet over its own strain grid, evaluates the ASE-calculator stress (eV/A^3) on each deformation, and does the linear fit ITSELF: _elastic_tensor_from_strains runs np.polyfit(strain, stress, 1) component-by-component to build C_ij, then ElasticTensor.from_voigt. VRH moduli (k_vrh, g_vrh) come from pymatgen; Young's E = 9KG/(3K+G) is computed in-matcalc from the VRH averages (matcalc bypasses pymatgen's y_mod because y_mod hardcodes a 9e9 GPa->Pa factor that is wrong for eV/A^3 storage, Issue #85).

schemes.use_equilibrium: bool (forced True if only one strain type)
schemes.relax_structure: bool (default True, pre-relax the parent cell)

entries.notes: THE central rail-vs-schemes exhibit. matcalc's fit IS physics (its strain grid is the operator discretization), but every OUTPUT node is already mapped (pymatgen scan grounds ElasticConstants/BulkModulus/ShearModulus; YoungsModulus is a pymatgen new-node-candidate). So encode matcalc as a scheme layer (OperatorRepresentationSpec) on compute_elastic_constants, carrying the strain grid + relax + equilibrium schemes, NOT as a rail. AtomisticSkills mat-elasticity: codes.json shows BulkModulus/ElasticConstants in GPa; the mlip scan says calculate_elasticity.py multiplies by 160.2176634 (CODATA-2018), consistent with matcalc's units_GPa=False default + skill-side conversion. DOUBLE the number of distinct strain conventions vs pymatgen: matcalc feeds ASE tension-positive eV/A^3 (no vasp=True -0.1 branch), resolving pymatgen open-question 5.

entries.api: EOSCalc(calc, optimizer='FIRE', max_abs_strain=0.1, n_points=11, fmax=0.1, allow_shape_change=True).calc(struct) -> {eos: {volumes[A^3], energies[eV]}, bulk_modulus_bm[GPa], r2_score_bm}
entries.physics_route: Birch-Murnaghan E(V). matcalc runs its OWN symmetric volumetric strain scan (n_points=11 over +/-max_abs_strain, apply_strain per point, fixed-VOLUME relaxation at each point via RelaxCalc with cell_filter_kwargs constant_volume=True), collects (V, E), then hands them to pymatgen BirchMurnaghan; bulk modulus is bm.b0_GPa.

schemes.allow_shape_change: relax cell shape at fixed volume

entries.notes: The E(V) energies are a TotalEnergy curve (per-cell eV); the BM bulk modulus is a SECOND bulk modulus distinct from the elastic-tensor VRH K (pymatgen scan already flags birch-murnaghan-eos as already-mapped, E0->per-cell TotalEnergy). matcalc's volume-scan grid is the operator scheme. AtomisticSkills mat-equation-of-state drives this (mlip scan).

entries.api: PhononCalc(calc, atom_disp=0.015, min_length=20.0, t_step=10, t_max=1000, fmax=1e-5).calc(struct) -> {phonon: Phonopy, thermal_properties, frequencies[THz], disp_supercells}
entries.physics_route: finite-difference phonons under phonopy. matcalc builds phonopy.Phonopy (supercell from min_length or supercell_matrix, primitive_matrix='auto'), generates displacements (atom_disp=0.015 A), evaluates ASE-calculator FORCES on each displaced supercell, produce_force_constants, run_mesh, run_thermal_properties. Frequencies are phonopy's mesh frequencies in linear THz; thermal_properties are phonopy's molar quantities.
entries.notes: matcalc DRIVES an ALREADY-MAPPED code (phonopy is a codes.json rail). The Frequency (linear THz) matches the map canonical linear-THz Frequency (pymatgen scan RESOLVED the linear-vs-angular question: canonical is linear THz). The molar thermal properties map to the existing MolarHelmholtzFreeEnergy/MolarEntropy/MolarHeatCapacity nodes (phonopy already grounds these). ForceConstants[order=2] via phonon.force_constants (write_force_constants). So no NEW nodes; matcalc's atom_disp/supercell/symprec are phonopy-representation schemes. AtomisticSkills mat-phonon + mat-solid-free-energy drive this.

entries.api: Phonon3Calc(calc, fc2_supercell=2x2x2, fc3_supercell=2x2x2, mesh_numbers=(20,20,20), fmax=0.1).calc(struct) -> {phonon3: Phono3py, temperatures[K], thermal_conductivity[W/(m*K)]}
entries.physics_route: three-phonon BTE (RTA) under phono3py. matcalc builds Phono3py, generates FC2 + FC3 displacements, evaluates ASE-calculator FORCES on each phonon and fc3 supercell, produce_fc2/produce_fc3, init_phph_interaction, run_thermal_conductivity. kappa is spatially averaged over the 3 diagonal components (kappa[..., :3].mean).
entries.notes: Drives ALREADY-MAPPED phono3py (codes.json rail grounds ThermalConductivity[bte_solver=rta] and ForceConstants[order=3] eV/A^3). Default RTA; is_LBTE=True (direct_inverse) reachable via thermal_conductivity_kwargs. AtomisticSkills mat-lattice-thermal-conductivity drives this. matcalc squeezes kappa to a scalar-per-T (loses the tensor the map's ThermalConductivity node carries) but the phono3py object retains the full tensor. Supercell/mesh are phono3py-representation schemes.

entries.api: MDCalc(calc, ensemble='nvt', temperature=300, timestep=1.0, steps=100, loginterval=1, trajfile=...).calc(struct) -> {trajectory: [ase.Atoms], potential_energy, kinetic_energy, total_energy, final_structure}
entries.physics_route: ASE molecular dynamics. matcalc initializes velocities (MaxwellBoltzmannDistribution), selects an ase.md integrator by ensemble (VelocityVerlet for nve; NoseHooverChainNVT/NVTBerendsen/Langevin/Andersen/Bussi for nvt*; NPT/NPTBerendsen/MTKNPT/... for npt*), attaches an ase.io.trajectory.TrajectoryWriter observer at loginterval, runs, reads all frames back. The trajectory is a list of ASE Atoms (positions + velocities per frame).
entries.notes: THE ASE-delta headline: ASE MD (via matcalc MDCalc) PRODUCES the map's Trajectory node (already in the MD tier, grounded by gpumd/lammps). ASE is the third engine that writes a Trajectory. The MD gauge (integrator, ensemble, thermostat, initial-condition noise) is the map's md_ensemble_noise gauge group. AtomisticSkills mat-sample-pes-by-md drives this (mlip scan). MDCalc also averages potential/kinetic/total energy over the trailing frames window.

entries.api: EnergeticsCalc(calc, elemental_refs='MatPES-PBE', use_gs_reference=False).calc(struct) -> {formation_energy_per_atom[eV/atom], cohesive_energy_per_atom[eV/atom or None]}
entries.physics_route: E_form = (E_relaxed - sum_el n_el * E_ref_per_atom(el)) / nsites. Reference per-atom energies come from a bundled elemental-refs library (MP-PBE / MatPES-PBE / MatPES-r2SCAN) either tabulated (use_gs_reference=True) or recomputed by relaxing the reference elemental structures with the SAME calculator (use_gs_reference=False, default). Cohesive energy uses per-atom isolated-atom references (energy_atomic).
entries.notes: Maps to the FormationEnergy node the pymatgen scan minted (per-atom eV/atom, intensive; NOT the per-cell TotalEnergy). matcalc computes it directly from MLIP energies + a functional-specific elemental reference set. The elemental_refs choice is a required PROVENANCE field (ties the formation energy to a functional generation). cohesive_energy_per_atom has no map node today (cohesive energy vs isolated atoms) but is a close relative; noted as a minor candidate.

entries.api: SurfaceCalc(calc, relax_bulk=True, relax_slab=True, fmax=0.1, optimizer='BFGS').calc_slabs(bulk, miller_index) -> per-slab {surface_energy}
entries.physics_route: gamma = (E_slab - N_slab * E_bulk_per_atom) / (2 * area), the slab-bulk energy difference over twice the slab area. matcalc generates slabs with pymatgen SlabGenerator, relaxes bulk (with cell) and slab (fixed cell), and forms the difference. In-matcalc gamma is in eV/A^2 (NO explicit J/m^2 conversion in SurfaceCalc; the skill converts).

units.surface_energy: eV/A^2 (matcalc native); skill converts to J/m^2 via 16.0218 per pymatgen scan

entries.notes: Maps to the SurfaceEnergy node the pymatgen scan minted (J/m^2). matcalc SurfaceCalc returns eV/A^2 (no unit conversion in the calc); the J/m^2 = 16.021766339999996 * eV/A^2 conversion (CODATA-2018) lives in the AtomisticSkills mat-surface-energy skill (pymatgen scan trap #7). Default optimizer here is BFGS (not FIRE).

entries.api: NEBCalc(calc, optimizer='BFGS', climb=True, method='improvedtangent', fmax=0.1).calc_images(start, end, n_images=7) -> {barrier[eV], force[eV/A], mep: MEP, converged}
entries.physics_route: nudged elastic band (CI-NEB). matcalc interpolates n_images between start and end structures, builds an ase.mep.NEB, optimizes with an ASE optimizer (default BFGS), and reads the barrier off ase NEBTools.get_barrier(); the MEP dataclass stores the per-image structures + energies.
entries.notes: The NEB migration barrier (eV) has NO dedicated node on the map today. It is the transition-state energy of a diffusion/migration path, related to but distinct from the Arrhenius ActivationEnergy (which the map has, fitted from D(T)). NEW-NODE candidate: a migration/NEB barrier. Not confirmed to be driven by a specific AtomisticSkills mat-* skill in the prior scans (mat-diffusion-analysis fits Arrhenius, not NEB); low priority, flag as an open question.

entries.producer: matcalc.QHACalc (drives phonopy.PhonopyQHA over a volume scan)
entries.api: QHACalc(calc, scale_factors=(0.95..1.05), eos='vinet', t_max=1000).calc(struct) -> {gibbs_free_energies[kJ/mol], thermal_expansion_coefficients[1/K], bulk_modulus_P[GPa], heat_capacity_P[J/(K*mol)], gruneisen_parameters}
entries.physics_route: quasi-harmonic approximation. matcalc runs PhononCalc at each of ~11 volume scale factors, feeds F(V,T)+Cv+S to phonopy.PhonopyQHA with an EOS model (vinet default), and reads Gibbs G(T), thermal expansion alpha(T), constant-P bulk modulus B(T), Cp(T), and macroscopic Gruneisen gamma(T).
entries.notes: QHA thermodynamics (Gibbs G(T), thermal expansion alpha(T), B(T), Cp(T)) are NOT on the map today; the map's mode-Gruneisen node is the per-mode harmonic quantity, whereas QHACalc's gruneisen_parameters is the macroscopic thermodynamic gamma(T) (a different, temperature-dependent quantity, distinct node). NEW-NODE domain (finite-T thermodynamics). Not confirmed driven by a specific mat-* skill in the prior scans; medium priority, flag as open question. The molar Cp(T) is a constant-PRESSURE analog of the map's constant-volume molar heat capacity.

maps_to.note: the ASE machinery matcalc rides on (representation detail, not a physics node)

entries.producer: ase.optimize (FIRE/BFGS), ase.filters (FrechetCellFilter), ase.constraints (FixAtoms/FixSymmetry), ase.md (integrators), ase.units (CODATA-2014), ase.io (Trajectory)
entries.physics_route: N/A: these are the ASE algorithm/representation surfaces (optimizer choice, cell filter, constraints, integrator, unit constants) that become SCHEMES on the matcalc operators, not independent physics-DAG nodes.
entries.notes: The ASE delta beyond Potential-hosting is REALIZED through these: FrechetCellFilter is how RelaxCalc relaxes the cell (the Structure producer); ase.md integrators + ase.io.Trajectory are how MDCalc produces the Trajectory; ase.units.GPa (CODATA-2014) is the unit generation matcalc's RAW stress rides on (vs matcalc/pymatgen CODATA-2018 at the analysis boundary). Representation-only: these are scheme/gauge inputs, not nodes. The map's single ase Potential entry should GAIN scheme vocabulary (optimizer in {FIRE, BFGS, ...}, cell_filter in {FrechetCellFilter, ...}, ensemble in the 13 MD variants) rather than new ase code entries.

unit_traps: matcalc elastic default is eV/A^3 (units_GPa=False), NOT GPa. A consumer treating ElasticityCalc's elastic_tensor / bulk_modulus_vrh as GPa without checking units_GPa is off by 160x. AtomisticSkills mat-elasticity converts skill-side (*160.2176634, CODATA-2018), consistent with the default.

THREE CODATA generations at matcalc boundaries: (a) raw stress rides ase.units.GPa = CODATA-2014 (160.21766208); (b) matcalc.units.eVA3ToGPa = scipy CODATA-2018 (160.21766339999996); (c) ElasticityCalc GPa mode uses pymatgen ElasticTensor.GPa_to_eV_A3 = CODATA-2018 (160.21766339999996). matcalc's analysis boundary is CODATA-2018 (pymatgen side); its PES boundary is CODATA-2014 (ASE side). ~1e-8 relative, physically negligible, but a real provenance split.

matcalc EOS bulk_modulus_bm (pymatgen BM b0_GPa, GPa) is a DIFFERENT bulk modulus from ElasticityCalc bulk_modulus_vrh (elastic-tensor VRH, eV/A^3 default). Same node tag bulk_modulus but two distinct physics routes (EOS curvature vs elastic tensor average).

matcalc run_ase calls atoms.get_stress(voigt=False), returning a 3x3 tensor (NOT ASE Voigt-6). ElasticityCalc feeds the 3x3 to pymatgen's fitter. Any consumer expecting a Voigt-6 off SimulationResult.stress gets a 3x3.

phonopy under matcalc: frequencies are LINEAR THz (matches map canonical). thermal_properties are MOLAR (free_energy kJ/mol, entropy/Cv J/K/mol), matching the existing Molar* nodes, NOT the per-mode nodes.

matcalc fmax conventions differ by calculator: RelaxCalc/ElasticityCalc/EOSCalc/Phonon3Calc/EnergeticsCalc/SurfaceCalc fmax=0.1 eV/A; PhononCalc/QHACalc fmax=1e-5 eV/A (tight pre-relax, phonons need a near-perfect equilibrium). SurfaceCalc/NEBCalc default optimizer BFGS; the rest default FIRE.
surface energy: SurfaceCalc.calc returns gamma in eV/A^2 (no J/m^2 conversion inside matcalc); the J/m^2 factor 16.021766339999996 (CODATA-2018) lives in the mat-surface-energy skill.
matcalc pressure inputs are in eV/A^3 for MDCalc (pressure = 1.01325*units.bar) but GPa for QHACalc (pressure kwarg documented GPa). Mixed pressure-unit conventions across calculators.
open_questions: AtomisticSkills repo is NOT on this machine. The exact matcalc kwargs each mat-* skill passes (units_GPa, fmax, ensemble, elemental_refs, strain grids) are anchored to the three prior scans + codes.json, not a live read. Re-run with the repo mounted to confirm.

Rail vs schemes for matcalc: recommendation is schemes-on-operators (OperatorRepresentationSpec, same as atomate2), because every matcalc output node is already rail-grounded (MLIP/phonopy/phono3py/pymatgen) even though matcalc's strain/EOS fits are genuine discretization physics. Orchestrator to confirm and decide WHERE the matcalc scheme + MLIP-checkpoint double-provenance lands.

ASE delta placement: the map's single ase Potential entry should gain scheme vocabulary (optimizer, cell_filter, ensemble) rather than new ase code entries. The Structure-via-relax and Trajectory-via-MD productions are ASE operations already grounded by other rails; confirm no new ase code entry is needed.

matcalc NEB barrier (eV) and QHA thermodynamics (Gibbs, thermal expansion, Cp(T), macroscopic Gruneisen(T)) are NEW-NODE candidates NOT confirmed driven by a specific mat-* skill in the prior scans. Decide whether to ingest these domains or leave them as matcalc-capability-only.
cohesive_energy_per_atom (EnergeticsCalc, eV/atom vs isolated atoms) has no map node; minor candidate distinct from FormationEnergy.

matcalc squeezes phono3py kappa to a scalar-per-T (diagonal mean), losing the tensor the map's ThermalConductivity node carries. Confirm the encode reads the full tensor off the phono3py object, not matcalc's squeezed scalar.

ase.units.GPa CODATA-2014 vs matcalc/pymatgen CODATA-2018 at the matcalc boundary: the same ~1e-8 in-repo constant split the pymatgen and VASP scans flagged, now with matcalc explicitly on the CODATA-2018 side. Record on the elastic/EOS/surface representations.
